name: data-lineage-viz
description: Visualize object dependencies and data lineage from .dacpac files or by importing from SQL Server, Azure SQL, Fabric DW, and Synapse databases

commands.title: Data Lineage: Create AI Output Templates
Data Lineage: Toggle Schema Overview Mode
Data Lineage: Show AI Trace in Graph

dataLineageViz.maxNodes.markdownDescription: Maximum number of database objects to load. The graph rendering is separately capped by `renderLimit`. Values above 1000 are supported for AI/data queries but the GUI will show schema overview or a limit warning.

dataLineageViz.renderLimit.markdownDescription: Maximum rendered nodes before showing a warning instead of the graph. Prevents browser performance issues with very large visualizations. AI tools and BFS operate on the full model regardless of this limit.

dataLineageViz.excludePatterns.markdownDescription: Regex patterns to permanently exclude objects by name at load time (e.g. `_TEST$`, `^staging\\.`). Matched against `schema.object`. Supports `%` wildcard. Requires reloading the data source to take effect. For interactive ad-hoc filtering without reload, use the Exclusion Rules toolbar button.

dataLineageViz.externalRefs.enabled.markdownDescription: Detect external references (OPENROWSET file paths, cross-database 3-part names). When disabled, no virtual external nodes are created. External Tables are unaffected.

dataLineageViz.overview.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable schema overview mode. When enabled and the graph exceeds `overview.threshold` nodes, the initial view shows schema-level bubbles instead of all objects. Set to `false` to always show the full object graph.

dataLineageViz.overview.threshold.markdownDescription: Node count above which schema overview mode auto-activates on initial load. Only applies when `overview.enabled` is `true`.

dataLineageViz.parseRulesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom parse rules YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/PARSE_RULES.md)

dataLineageViz.dmvQueryTimeout.markdownDescription: Timeout (seconds) per DMV query. Increase for large databases.

dataLineageViz.dmvQueriesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom DMV queries YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/DMV_QUERIES.md)

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.enabled.markdownDescription: Show column statistics and row counts in the table design viewer (database import only).

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.standardM.markdownDescription: Allow **Standard** profiling mode (adds AVG, STDEV, zero/empty counts). When `false`, only Quick mode is available — lighter queries.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.excludeEx.markdownDescription: Skip profiling for external tables. External tables query remote data sources (S3, Blob, other databases) — profiling can be slow and expensive.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleThr.markdownDescription: Advanced: Tables above this row count use sampling instead of a full scan. Set to `0` to always sample.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleSiz.markdownDescription: Advanced: Number of rows to sample when profiling large tables.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.useApprox.markdownDescription: Advanced: Use `APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT` instead of exact `COUNT(DISTINCT)`. Much faster with ~2% margin. Requires SQL Server 2019+.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.maxColumn.markdownDescription: Advanced: Maximum number of columns to profile per table. Columns beyond this cap are skipped. Prevents massive queries on wide tables.

dataLineageViz.layout.direction.markdownDescription: Direction of the dependency graph layout.

enumDescriptions: Smooth step edges with rounded corners

dataLineageViz.layout.edgeAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when running a trace.

dataLineageViz.layout.highlightAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when clicking a node.

dataLineageViz.layout.minimapEnabled.markdownDescription: Show the graph minimap for navigation.

dataLineageViz.layout.rankSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Horizontal spacing between dependency layers (pixels).

dataLineageViz.layout.nodeSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Vertical spacing between nodes in the same layer (pixels).

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultUpstreamLeve.markdownDescription: How many levels upstream (inputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultDownstreamLe.markdownDescription: How many levels downstream (outputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.analysis.hubMinDegree.markdownDescription: Minimum number of connections for an object to appear in the hub list.

dataLineageViz.analysis.islandMaxSize.markdownDescription: Max connected component size to qualify as an island. Higher values include larger disconnected subgraphs; set lower (e.g. 5) to focus on small isolated pairs/groups only.

dataLineageViz.analysis.longestPathMinNo.markdownDescription: Minimum number of objects in a dependency chain for longest-path analysis.

dataLineageViz.ai.enabled.description: Enable the @lineage chat participant and AI lineage tools. Disable for users who do not use VS Code Copilot or want to remove AI features entirely.

dataLineageViz.ai.maxRounds.markdownDescription: Maximum tool-call rounds per `@lineage` request. Increase for complex explorations on deep graphs. Each round is one AI reasoning step + tool call.

dataLineageViz.ai.inlineTokenBudget.markdownDescription: Maximum estimated tokens for inline (quick) delivery. When a trace or exploration scope fits within **both** this token budget and the node cap, the AI receives all SQL at once for faster analysis. Exceeding either threshold switches to hop-by-hop deep exploration with persistent memory. Lower values favor accuracy; higher values favor speed.

dataLineageViz.ai.inlineNodeCap.markdownDescription: Maximum scope nodes for inline (quick) delivery. When a trace or exploration scope has more nodes than this, the AI explores hop-by-hop with persistent memory for deeper analysis. Deep mode tracks column renames and transformations across many dependency stages. Lower values favor accuracy; higher values favor speed.

dataLineageViz.ai.outputTemplateFile.markdownDescription: Path to custom AI output templates YAML. Controls how `@lineage` formats summary, description, badges, highlights, and notes. Leave empty for built-in defaults. Use Command Palette → *Create AI Output Templates* to scaffold. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/AI_PROMPTS.md)

dataLineageViz.ai.showToolInvocations.markdownDescription: Show each `@lineage` tool call as an expandable chat part (with input JSON). When off (default), the copy button captures only the AI prose; tool progress is shown ephemerally via status text and full tool I/O is available in the **Data Lineage Viz** output channel. Turn on for developer debugging — note that VS Code's built-in chat copy will then include tool-call details.

chatParticipants.name: lineage
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions about your loaded lineage graph — tables, procedures, dependencies, data flow.

commands.name: trace
commands.description: Trace data lineage for an object or column

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search for database objects by name or schema

disambiguation.description: Questions about database dependencies, data flow, stored procedures, table lineage, SQL object relationships, where data comes from or goes to
examples: Trace the data flow for orders
What tables does this procedure read from
Show me the lineage for the financehub schema
What are the upstream sources of this table

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_context
languageModelTools.userDescription: Shows what is currently loaded and visible in the lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current database context including loaded schemas, stats, and active UI filters.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_objects
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for database objects by name or column.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search database objects by name or column name using substring or regex matching. Returns object IDs and metadata. Each result carries an `in_user_filter` flag — when an in-filter search returns 0 hits but out-of-filter results exist, include that schema in the next search; the active filter is a display preference, not a boundary.

query.description: Object name or column name to search for (max 200 chars). Do not include schema prefixes like '[dbo].' here.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_object_detail
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get full details for a specific database object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns full metadata for a single object, including columns, foreign keys, DDL, and immediate neighbors. DISCOVERY and SYNTHESIS phases only — during active SM exploration, use lineage_get_neighbor_columns instead.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_neighbor_columns
languageModelTools.userDescription: Inspect a neighbor's columns for pruning decisions during active SM exploration.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns structural metadata (columns, types, nullability, foreign keys) for one or more direct neighbors of the current focus node. Use during active SM exploration to decide whether to prune a neighbor (e.g. when the focus DDL uses 'SELECT *' and you need the neighbor's column list). NEVER returns DDL bodies. Pass neighbor ids only — do NOT include the focus node id itself; neighbors must also be within the active BFS scope.

ids.description: One or more direct-neighbor node IDs to inspect (e.g. ['[dbo].[FactSales]']).

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_neighborhood
languageModelTools.userDescription: Fetch the surrounding dependency graph for an object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the upstream and/or downstream neighbors for a starting object within a specified depth.

schemas.description: Include only nodes in these schemas.

target.description: Path mode: find all nodes on paths from id to target. When provided, upstream_hops/downstream_hops are ignored.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_detect_graph_patterns
languageModelTools.userDescription: Find structural patterns like hubs, cycles, or orphans in the graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyzes the entire graph for specific structural patterns such as hubs, islands, orphans, longest paths, cycles, or external references.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_ddl
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search SQL body scripts for a text pattern.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Performs a regex search across view, procedure, and function DDL bodies. Returns matching lines with context.

query.description: Regex pattern to search for in DDL bodies.

types.description: Limit search to these object types.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_present_result
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate a final report and visualization for the exploration.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Authors the presentation layer (report) for an exploration result. Groups nodes into sections and provides an executive summary.

summary.description: One-line digest for the info card.

title.description: Optional. Heading for the description panel.

intro.description: Optional. Context paragraph shown before sections.

closing.description: Optional. Closing note or risk assessment.

prune_node_ids.description: Nodes to remove from the final visualization.

highlight_groups.description: Optional. Highlight groups for critical nodes.

sections.description: Report sections grouping nodes with explanatory text.

is_update.description: Set true to update an existing view.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_start_exploration
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start an autonomous exploration of database objects for data flow, business rules, or investigations.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Starts a hop-by-hop exploration across database objects (Blackboard, or Column-Trace when `targetColumns` is set). Scope parameters: pick `direction`, `depth`, `depth_enforcement`, and `excludeTypes` to match how the user phrased scope. Honor NL filters: 'ignore UDFs/functions' → `excludeTypes:['function']`; 'ignore views' → `['view']`; 'only tables' → `['view','procedure','function']`. On wide graphs start `depth` shallow (1-2); sliding-memory sessions lock scope at session start, and out-of-scope routes are deferred to a post-session review list rather than prompting mid-session. Compose `mission_brief` (3-6 sentences) capturing user intent, NL filters, name-based exclusions, and chosen scope — delivered verbatim every hop and surviving memory wipes. POST-SYNTHESIS SUPPLEMENT: in the follow-up phase, call with only `supplement:{nodeIds:[...]}` (no `origin` required) to extend the existing archive with one-shot inline analysis for those ids. The prior engine, memory, and filter state are reused — new slots merge into the archive; a fresh re-render via `lineage_present_result` follows. Use this path for deferred-question continuations and user-requested 'add node X' refinements.

## Decision order

When the user refines a pending `confirm_sm_start` gate, settle decisions in this order — later axes depend on earlier ones being fixed:

1. **Origin** — locked. Refine cannot change origin; that is a fresh question. If the user names a different starting object, ask them to cancel and re-ask.
2. **Direction** (`upstream` / `downstream` / `bidirectional`) — refinable. Re-runs BFS.
3. **Depth** (`depth`, `depth_enforcement`) — refinable. Re-runs BFS.
4. **Per-node bucket** for each node BFS surfaced — analyze (default) / pass-through / prune.
5. **Mode + classification + tracing** — `forceMode`, `classification`, `targetColumns`.

## Scope filters

`excludeSchemas`, `excludeTypes`, `excludeNodeIds`, and `passNodeIds` are applied at BFS scope-build time. Each call REPLACES the prior filter set on the engine — accumulate across rounds by sending every prior filter plus the new one in a single call.

## Pass vs prune

A node has two narrowing semantics. Choose deliberately:

- **`passNodeIds`** — node stays in scope; BFS still routes through it (descendants reachable); the engine auto-emits `verdict:'pass'` so no analysis is written for that node. Topology preserved. Default interpretation when the user says 'ignore', 'skip', or 'don't analyze X'.
- **`excludeNodeIds`** / **`excludeSchemas`** / **`excludeTypes`** — node (and its subtree reachable only through it) is cut at BFS time. Use only when the user explicitly says 'remove', 'drop', 'prune', 'cut', or names a leaf they reject.

If you propose pruning a node that would orphan in-scope descendants, rewrite the call as `passNodeIds` for those ids unless the user has explicitly approved removing the subtree.

## Discovery-phase refinement loop

When a `confirm_sm_start` gate is pending and the user wants to adjust the proposed scope:

1. Read the user's chat message and map their intent to the right axis using the decision order above. Phrasing varies — they may say ignore / drop / remove / without / no / skip — and they may name schemas, object types, or specific objects. Use your judgment; if intent is unclear, ask one short clarifying question and call no tool that turn.
2. Call `lineage_start_exploration` with the same `origin` from the prior call, plus the updated `direction` / `depth` / `excludeTypes` / `excludeSchemas` / `excludeNodeIds` / `passNodeIds` / `forceMode` / `classification` / `targetColumns` as the user requested.
3. The engine re-runs BFS, re-evaluates mode (inline / sliding-memory, blackboard / column-trace), and re-emits the gate with the new counts. The user reviews and either approves, refines further, or cancels.
4. During this loop, only `lineage_start_exploration` is appropriate — `submit_findings` is not yet available, and analysis begins only after the user approves.

<examples>
<example>
User: 'ignore the staging schema'
Call: lineage_start_exploration({ origin: <kept>, depth: <kept>, excludeSchemas: ['staging'] })
</example>

<example>
Prior call excluded 'staging'. User: 'also drop procedures.'
Call: lineage_start_exploration({ origin: <kept>, depth: <kept>, excludeSchemas: ['staging'], excludeTypes: ['procedure'] })
</example>

<example>
User: 'ignore usp_PostJournal but I still want to see what it reads from'
Call: lineage_start_exploration({ origin: <kept>, depth: <kept>, passNodeIds: ['[dbo].[usp_PostJournal]'] })
</example>

<example>
User: 'remove the staging subtree entirely — I don\'t need anything below it'
Call: lineage_start_exploration({ origin: <kept>, depth: <kept>, excludeSchemas: ['staging'] })
</example>

<example>
Prior gate: Mode=Inline, Analysis=business. User: 'do this as sliding memory and trace TotalRevenue'
Call: lineage_start_exploration({ origin: <kept>, depth: <kept>, forceMode: 'sm', targetColumns: ['TotalRevenue'] })
</example>
</examples>

targetColumns.description: Optional. Specific column names to trace (activates Column Trace mode).

direction.description: BFS direction for initial scope discovery.

depth_enforcement.description: How to treat the depth budget: 'strict' (hard cap), 'soft' (+1 depth expansion allowed), or 'silent' (+2 depth allowed). Default 'silent'.

schemas.description: Optional. Override active filter to search specific schemas.

mission_brief.description: A 3-6 sentence natural-language briefing distilling user intent, NL filters, scope choices, and pruning criteria.

excludeTypes.description: Optional. Object types to prune from the exploration scope. Use whenever the user names a type-level filter in NL — e.g. 'no views' / 'exclude all views' / 'tables only' / 'skip UDFs' map to ['view'] / ['view'] / ['view','procedure','function'] / ['function'] respectively. ALSO honor the GUI filter as the user's default: when `filter_context.active_types` from a prior search result lists e.g. ['table','procedure'], the user has already hidden ['view','function'] in the GUI — include those in `excludeTypes` unless the user's chat explicitly asks to broaden ('include views', 'also look at UDFs'). The gate shows the resulting scope; if the user wants to widen, they'll say so on refine. REPLACE semantics — each call overrides prior. Cumulative across refine rounds (re-send all prior plus the new).

excludeSchemas.description: Optional. Schemas to prune from the exploration scope (case-insensitive). REPLACE semantics. Use when the user says 'remove/drop schema X' or 'ignore X-schema objects'. ALSO honor `filter_context.active_schemas` from a prior search result as the user's GUI default: any schema present in the loaded model but missing from `active_schemas` is currently hidden — include it here unless the user's chat explicitly asks to broaden ('also look at STAGING').

excludeNodeIds.description: Optional. Specific node ids to prune from the BFS scope (case-insensitive). Cuts the node and any subtree reachable only through it. Use when the user says 'remove/drop/cut object X'. REPLACE semantics.

passNodeIds.description: Optional. Node ids to keep in scope but auto-pass — engine emits verdict:'pass' so no analysis is written, topology preserved so descendants stay reachable. Default interpretation when the user says 'ignore/skip/don't analyze X'. REPLACE semantics.

forceMode.description: Optional. Override the inline-vs-SM mode heuristic. Use only when the user explicitly says 'do this inline' or 'force sliding memory'. After the gate is approved, the chosen mode locks in.

classification.description: Declare the analysis angle. Default `both` when intent is ambiguous (general lineage / origin / impact / 'where does X come from' / 'what reads from Y' — including column traces) — engine captures one business + one technical section per node, giving the full picture. Pick `business` only when the user explicitly wants only business rules. Pick `technical` only when the user explicitly asks about performance, execution plans, indexes, query tuning, statistics, or data-quality / null-handling semantics.

supplement.description: Post-synthesis supplement. Supply only in the follow-up phase to extend the existing archive with additional ids. When present, `origin` may be omitted. Runs the existing engine one-shot in inline mode; new slots merge into the archive.

nodeIds.description: Node ids to add to the existing exploration (typically picked from `deferred_questions`).

languageModelTools.name: lineage_submit_findings
languageModelTools.userDescription: Submit analysis of the current node and propose next routes in the exploration.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submits analysis for the current focus node and requests the next hop's data. The `sections` array carries one captured section per fired *_capture template — `business` classification → 1 business section; `technical` → 1 technical; `both` → 2 sections (one of each). The locked classification is enforced mechanically; mismatches are rejected with a structured hint.

focus_node_id.description: ID of the current focus node.

angle.description: Which capture template produced this section.

text.description: Pre-formatted section body — written per the angle's *_capture instruction. Lifted verbatim by synthesis.

sections.description: One section per fired *_capture template. Length 1 (business OR technical classification) or 2 (both). May be empty for verdict=prune.

summary.description: One short sentence digest of the whole node — echoed in future hops via short_term_memory.

verdict.description: Action for focus node: 'analyze' (store findings), 'pass' (mark visited), or 'prune' (block branch).

nodeId.description: Exact, schema-qualified neighbor ID to visit (e.g. `[schema].[name]`). Must come VERBATIM from a prior tool result's `next_hop` / `neighbors[]` data, a `lineage_get_neighbor_columns` lookup, or a `lineage_search_objects` result. Inventing or guessing an id (even a small typo) causes a `route_validation_failed` rejection — the rejection envelope returns up to 3 fuzzy candidates per unresolved id; reuse the matching candidate verbatim, or call `lineage_search_objects` with the un-bracketed name to find the real id.

columns.description: Specific columns to follow in next hop.

route_requests.description: Routing requests for next hops. Every `nodeId` must already exist in the loaded model (verified against a prior tool result or via `lineage_search_objects`); unresolved ids are rejected and do not enter the agenda.

prune_neighbors.description: Neighbor node IDs to aggressively prune from the agenda.

badge_label.description: Short semantic role (2-4 words) for node badge (e.g. 'Source', 'Transformation').

note_caption.description: One-line technical reasoning caption shown on the node.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.build: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.test: tsx tests/unit/dacpacExtractor.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/graphBuilder.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/parser-edge-cases.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/graphAnalysis.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/graph-analysis-aw.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/dmvExtractor.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/tsql-complex.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/projectStore.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/snapshot-aw-baseline.ts && tsx tests/unit/ai-tool-registration.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/sm-robustness.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/classification.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/session-stale.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/start-exploration-schema.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/chatResponseWriter.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/messageEnvelope.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/refine-loop.test.ts
scripts.test:unit:ai: tsx tests/unit/ai-tools.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/navigation-engine.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/navigation-engine-cascade.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/navigation-engine-bipartite.test.ts && tsx tests/unit/navigation-engine-supplement.test.ts
